personas.name: Users on a budget
personas.description: A user who uses Medito as a new user or return from other meditation apps that are free to use.
goals: Try out content before deciding to donate
Seek transparent free access without forced payment
pain_points: Donation prompts can feel like a paywall
Users may be skeptical of free access without clear value
Competing apps interrupt the experience with subscriptions or ads
context: Evaluating Medito after using or comparing paid meditation apps
Looking for a low-cost wellness tool that still feels polished
Willing to donate after value is proven
constraints: Session playback must remain available without ads or interruptions

personas.name: Bedtime Calming Listener
personas.description: Users who uses Medito at night to fall asleep or get back to sleep and needs bedtime content with uninterrupted playback.
goals: Keep calming audio playing at bedtime
pain_points: Playback delays or interruptions break the bedtime routine
Repeat playback can be interrupted by streak or completion prompts
One session is not always enough to fall asleep
context: Using the app in bed, often with the screen dimmed or off
Returning to the app after waking during the night
Sometimes putting on content for children or family members
constraints: Playback should continue without overly bright or blocking overlays at night
Sleep audio should be accessible with very few taps

personas.name: Users who seek emotional reset
personas.description: Users turning to Medito during anxiety, stress, depression, grief, or panic who need calming support.
goals: Find sessions that match the current emotional need
Calm down quickly during difficult moments
Build focus and peace during stressful periods
pain_points: It can be hard to choose the right session under stress
Some guide voices do not feel calming to every listener
Users need support that feels dependable during crisis moments
context: Using the app during school, work, grief, or panic episodes
Sometimes trying meditation for emotional regulation for the first time
Needing guided help rather than open-ended meditation
constraints: Need-based content must be easy to identify
Calming support must be reachable without extra setup

personas.name: The Habit Builder
personas.description: For new or returning meditators using courses, short daily practice, and streaks to build consistency.
goals: Get reminded back into practice at the same time each day
pain_points: Beginners do not always know where to start
Consistency is harder without reminders or visible progress
Returning users want the app to point them to the next step
context: Starting meditation for the first time or after a long break
Using short daily sessions and beginner courses
Relying on streaks or challenges for motivation
constraints: Onboarding must point to the next lesson clearly
Habit-building tools should require minimal setup

personas.name: Power Users
personas.description: Frequent users who expect audio to function properly, search, and progress tracking to maintain after interruptions, updates, and device changes.
goals: Complete sessions without crashes or audio cutouts
Preserve streaks and course progress across devices
Use account actions without losing data
pain_points: Playback can crash, pause, or stop unexpectedly
Progress can disappear after unexpected crashes, updates, sign-in issues, or device changes
Search or sign-out can feel risky when data is involved
context: Using the app daily and across multiple devices
Listening with headphones, screen off, or offline downloads
Noticing regressions after updates because the app is part of a daily routine
constraints: Playback must remain stable through common interruptions
Synced progress must be recoverable after sign-in on another device
